title: The Astrologer Who Fell into a Well
lines: An Astrologer was walking at night, gazing up at the stars and making calculations.
He was so intent on the heavens that he did not watch where his feet were going.
He fell headlong into a deep well that lay in his path.
His cries brought people running to his aid.
'You claim to read the future in the stars," they said as they pulled him out,
'yet you cannot see what lies at your feet.'
moral: Do not neglect what is near while pursuing distant dreams.
